articles.title: Iran attacks US bases in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan in retaliation for US strikes near Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The IRGC said it launched drone attacks on the US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain and the Ali Al Salem airbase in Kuwait, and a long-range missile strike on an airbase in Azraq, Jordan, in retaliation for US strikes on Iranian air defence and radar sites near the Strait of Hormuz. Authorities in all three countries said the projectiles were intercepted with no casualties reported.

articles.title: US strikes Iranian air defences, radar sites and Qeshm Island after accusing Iran of downing Apache helicopter
articles.summary: The US military struck Iranian air defence, ground control stations and surveillance radar sites near the Strait of Hormuz, after earlier attacks on Qeshm Island and ports along the Iranian coast. Washington said the strikes were a response to Iran downing a US Apache helicopter on Tuesday. Tehran claimed the strikes in the south damaged the water supply for thousands.

articles.title: Trump threatens fresh strikes on Iranian power plants and bridges, says Iran 'will have to pay the price' for stalled talks
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump said he may order new strikes on Iran's power plants and bridges because Tehran is taking too long to make a deal. The IRGC warned its forces remain fully prepared to deliver a 'crushing and decisive' response to any US military action.

articles.title: Qatari negotiators travel to Tehran to finalize US-Iran ceasefire agreement
articles.summary: Qatari negotiators travelled to Tehran on Wednesday morning in an effort to finalize a US-Iran ceasefire agreement, after consultations with the United States. Trump separately said Israel and Iran were 'looking to do an immediate ceasefire.'

articles.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 13 in southern Lebanon as IDF presses Hezbollah campaign; Lebanon reports 30 dead over past day
articles.summary: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 13 people on Wednesday, including nine in the village of Dayr Debba east of Tyre, as Israel pressed its campaign against Hezbollah. Lebanon's health ministry said 30 people were killed over the past day. Strikes on Tyre on Tuesday killed 11 amid evacuation warnings for the entire city.

articles.title: Israeli defense officials warn Iran is succeeding in linking the Iran and Lebanon fronts, limiting IDF freedom of action
articles.summary: Israeli defense officials warn that Iran's strategy of tying its responses to Israeli operations in Lebanon is limiting the IDF's freedom of action, as Tehran conditions regional de-escalation on a halt to strikes in southern Lebanon.

articles.title: Trump's Israel-Iran 'timeout' holds, but Lebanese impasse threatens renewed fighting
articles.summary: After a renewed flare-up, a temporary hiatus in Israel-Iran fighting has been achieved at Trump's request, but the absence of any agreement on the Lebanese front threatens to drag the region into another escalation. Israel says it will continue its Lebanon offensive 'at full force.'

articles.title: Israel reopens Kerem Shalom and Rafah crossings after sealing Gaza during Iran exchange
articles.summary: Israel announced the reopening of Gaza's crossings after sealing them during the latest exchange of fire with Iran: Kerem Shalom for the gradual entry of humanitarian aid and Rafah for limited movement of people in both directions. IDF strikes in Gaza were reported to have killed six people.

articles.title: Cairo talks on Gaza ceasefire's second phase continue as Hamas resists full disarmament
articles.summary: Mediators Egypt, Qatar and Turkey are meeting Palestinian factions in Cairo on the second phase of the Israel-Hamas ceasefire. Hamas signalled it would hand governance to a technocratic national committee but would not surrender its arms outright, tying decommissioning to an Israeli withdrawal; Israel and Board of Peace envoy Nickolay Mladenov have conditioned the next phase on disarmament.

articles.title: Knesset advances quasi-constitutional bill effectively equating Torah study with IDF service
articles.summary: The Knesset advanced a quasi-constitutional Basic Law amendment that would effectively equate Torah study with military service, a key demand of Haredi parties, as fresh Haredi anti-draft protests took place nationwide with several arrests.
